attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.

Symbol sprites are loaded dynamically by filename convention `Symbol_XX` from the game bundle (`GameView.LoadSymbol`).\\n\\n### Networking / protocol\\n\\n`Proto.ts` re-exports a generated protobufjs module from `assets/Script/Test/ar2lpProto.js` (despite the `Test` folder name, this is the live protocol used at runtime — not a test fixture). `GameView.OnCommand()` is the `ConnectionManager` callback that decodes incoming buffers (`info`, `spin`/`lastSpin`, `unshow`) into typed `ar2lpProto.*` messages. `RecoverSpinAck.ts` normalizes/converts raw server plate data for the reel components.\\n\\n`Game_Define.USE_MOCK_SERVER = true` makes `GameView.GenerateMockSpinAck()` fabricate spin results locally instead of talking to a real backend — check this flag before assuming network behavior in dev builds.\\n\\n### Presentation layers\\n\\n- `EffectPlate/` — board win presentation (win frames, symbol spine animations) — see `EffectPlate.ts`, `EffectSpine.ts`, `SymbolSpine.ts`.\\n- `Spine/` — reusable spine playback helpers (`BaseSpine`, `InLoopOutSpine`).\\n- `FreeGame/` — free-game enter/exit declaration presentation.\\n- `Audio/AudioManager.ts` — sound/BGM playback (see its own `使用說明.md`).\\n- `Common/` — generic helpers (node pooling, tweened numbers/sprites, roulette rotation, async utils).\\n\\n### Framework vs. game code\\n\\nAnything imported from `db://astarte-framework/...` (managers like `newBottombarManager`, `newExtraManager`, `msgBoxManager`, `stateManager`, `commonGameManager`, `eventManager`, `ConnectionManager`, shared `Define`/`CommonState`/`tools`) is shared, cross-project framework code living in the separately-versioned `extensions/astarte-framework` repo. Game-specific code under `assets/Script/` should consume that framework's public API, not modify it in place.\\n\",\"numLines\":72,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":72}}"</outcome>

SCATTER_SHOW / ADD_FREE / LEAVE_FREE）`。`GameView.ForEndToNext()` 負責判斷 round-end 之後要繼續下一輪免費遊戲的 spin，還是回到 `IDLE`。\\n\\n新增狀態時：在 `Game_Define.ts` 的 `GAMEVIEW_STATE` 加上列舉值 → 在 `GameState/` 建立對應的狀態類別 → 在 `GameView.SetStateMachine()` 註冊。\\n\\n### 轉輪與補位策略（Standard / Cascade / Tumble）\\n\\n`SlotReels.ts`（`SlotReel` component）持有轉輪的每一列（`ColumnSymbol` → `SingleCell`），並把停輪後的表演邏輯委派給可替換的 `IFillStrategy`（放在 `FillStrategy/`），透過 `SetSpinMode(SpinMode.Standard | Cascade | Tumble, strategy)` 切換：\\n\\n- **Standard**：停輪後直接報獎。\\n- **Cascade**/**Tumble**：停輪後反覆執行 `EXPLODE ↔ MATCHING_PATCH_UP`（消除中獎符號、上方符號天降補位）直到無可消除，才報獎。Cascade 是逐格掉落（適合中獎線玩法）；Tumble 是整列同步掉落（適合全盤消除／Ways 玩法）。\\n\\n各補位策略都實作固定的三階段 `fillColumn`：存活符號下沉 → 借用已消除的 cell 承載新符號掉入 → 呼叫 `ColumnSymbol.RearrangeSingleCells()` 把 cell 陣列索引與新的視覺位置對齊。若跳過重排這一步，下一輪的 explode 判定就會跟畫面對不上。完整說明、建構子可調參數與 DevTool console 指令（`ReelDevTool`）請見 `assets/Script/FillStrategy/使用說明.md`。\\n\\n盤面結構（列數/行數、各模式的可見與緩衝符號數量）由 `ReelLayoutConfig.ts` 資料驅動 → `REEL_LAYOUT_PRESETS.standard`（三層滾動式轉輪）／`.dropEntry`（單層，符號從上方掉入），需在 `CreateSymbol()` 之前透過 相關調整參數，以及其他各種時間常數。Symbol 圖片依 `Symbol_XX` 檔名慣例從遊戲 bundle 動態載入（見 `GameView.LoadSymbol`）。\\n\\n### 連線 / 協定\\n\\n`Proto.ts` 重新匯出 `assets/Script/Test/ar2lpProto.js` 這支由 protobufjs 產生的模組（雖然放在 `Test` Cascade drops per-cell (line-win games); Tumble drops per-column in sync (full-board/Ways games).\\n\\nFill strategies implement a fixed three-phase `fillColumn`: alive-symbols sink → new symbols drop in (via reused \\\"dead\\\" cells) → `ColumnSymbol.RearrangeSingleCells()` re-syncs the cell array to the new visual order. Skipping the rearrange step desyncs the next explode pass. See `assets/Script/FillStrategy/使用說明.md` for the full guide, constructor tuning options, and DevTool console commands (`ReelDevTool`).\\n\\nBoard layout (row/column counts, visible vs. buffered symbol counts per layout mode) is data-driven via `ReelLayoutConfig.ts` → `REEL_LAYOUT_PRESETS.standard` (3-layer scrolling reel) / `.dropEntry` (1-layer, symbols drop in) — set once via `SlotReel.SetLayoutConfig()` before `CreateSymbol()`.\\n\\n### Symbols\\n\\n`Game_Define.ts` is the central config: the `Symbol` enum (WILD, paytable letters/majors, CASH/JP tiers, SCATTER variants, FEATURE symbols), board dimensions (`COL`/`ROW`), the `FEATURES` flag map (which game mechanics are enabled), NearWin/Scatter tuning tables, and misc timing constants. Symbol sprites are loaded dynamically by filename convention `Symbol_XX` from the game bundle (`GameView.LoadSymbol`).\\n\\n### Networking / protocol\\n\\n`Proto.ts` re-exports a generated protobufjs module from `assets/Script/Test/ar2lpProto.js` (despite the `Test` folder name, this is the live protocol used at runtime — not a test fixture). `GameView.OnCommand()` is the `ConnectionManager` callback that decodes incoming buffers (`info`, `spin`/`lastSpin`, `unshow`) into typed `ar2lpProto.*` messages. `RecoverSpinAck.ts` normalizes/converts raw server plate data for the reel components.\\n\\n`Game_Define.USE_MOCK_SERVER = true` makes `GameView.GenerateMockSpinAck()` fabricate spin results locally instead of talking to a real backend — check this flag before assuming network behavior in dev builds.\\n\\n### Presentation layers\\n\\n- `EffectPlate/` — board win presentation (win frames, symbol spine animations) — see `EffectPlate.ts`, `EffectSpine.ts`, `SymbolSpine.ts`.\\n- `Spine/` — reusable spine playback helpers (`BaseSpine`, `InLoopOutSpine`).\\n- `FreeGame/` — free-game enter/exit declaration presentation.\\n- `Audio/AudioManager.ts` — sound/BGM playback (see its own `使用說明.md`).\\n- `Common/` — generic helpers (node pooling, tweened numbers/sprites, roulette rotation, async utils).\\n\\n### Framework vs. game code\\n\\nAnything imported from `db://astarte-framework/...` (managers like `newBottombarManager`, `newExtraManager`, `msgBoxManager`, `stateManager`, `commonGameManager`, `eventManager`, `ConnectionManager`, shared `Define`/`CommonState`/`tools`) is shared, cross-project framework code living in the separately-versioned `extensions/astarte-framework` repo. Game-specific code under `assets/Script/` should consume that framework's public API, not modify it in place.\\n\",\"userModified\":false}"</outcome>
